hero.title: The strategic fight is shifting from who has the best model to who owns the workflow around it.
hero.lede: Assess where strategic value is concentrating as model quality converges. As raw model capability converges, the battle for value is moving up the stack toward workflow ownership, context capture, and distribution.

topLine.title: Distribution is becoming as important as the model.
topLine.body: As raw model capability converges, the battle for value is moving up the stack toward workflow ownership, context capture, and distribution. Owning the user relationship becomes economically more important. Memory and workflow embedding can become part of the moat.

items.label: Distribution
items.text: Distribution is carrying more strategic weight as raw model differentiation narrows.

items.label: Workflow
items.text: Context capture is emerging as the scarcer asset than inference alone.

items.label: Product
items.text: Interface design is increasingly shaping product economics, not just usability.

items.title: 1. Distribution is carrying more strategic weight as raw model differentiation narrows
items.body: Multiple product and buyer signals point toward packaging, workflow integration, and reach becoming more decisive than standalone model access. Owning the user relationship becomes economically more important. Go-to-market strength can compound technical capability rather than merely follow it. Counterpoint: Frontier model gaps can still matter in narrow, high-stakes domains.

items.title: 2. Context capture is emerging as the scarcer asset than inference alone
items.body: Embedded systems that learn user habits and store reusable context are harder to displace than point solutions that only answer prompts. Memory and workflow embedding can become part of the moat. Switching costs may rise at the interface layer rather than the model layer.

items.title: 3. Interface design is increasingly shaping product economics, not just usability
items.body: The interface determines what gets repeated, measured, remembered, and eventually monetized. The presentation surface influences retention and monetization. UI decisions can determine where strategic capture lands.

marketMap.title: Who benefits if this thesis is right

items.label: Winners
items.text: Platforms and products that own distribution, recurring workflow, and durable user context.

items.label: Pressured
items.text: Vendors whose differentiation depends mainly on standalone access to a better model.

items.label: Opportunity
items.text: Vertical operators that can turn AI into embedded workflow and capture non-portable context over time.
Which layer captures the recurring user relationship?
What kinds of context become least portable over time?
Which products turn occasional use into operational dependency?
Which layer keeps the recurring user relationship as model access broadens?
Where will the most valuable non-portable context accumulate?
